pr.title: Split out lbarchive, lbdvd, and gmmain
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Assembly-only organization PR that split a large lb assembly translation unit into source-named files for lbarchive and lbdvd, left the remaining later-address code in code_80019230.s, and renamed the generic text_1.s blob to gm/gmmain.s. obj_files.mk was updated so the build links lbarchive.o, lbdvd.o, code_80019230.o, and gm/gmmain.o in place of the older monolithic/generic objects.
what_changed: Renamed asm/melee/text_1.s to asm/melee/gm/gmmain.s with 100% similarity, replacing a generic text blob name with a subsystem/source-style name.
Renamed asm/melee/lb/code_80016A54.s to asm/melee/lb/code_80019230.s and removed most of its earlier contents, leaving a small residual file for the remaining address range.
Added asm/melee/lb/lbarchive.s with 780 lines split out from the old lb code blob; the diff contains the source filename string "lbarchive.c".
Added asm/melee/lb/lbdvd.s with 2481 lines split out from the old lb code blob; the diff contains the source filename string "lbdvd.c".
Updated obj_files.mk to replace code_80016A54.o with lbarchive.o, lbdvd.o, and code_80019230.o, and to replace text_1.o with gm/gmmain.o.
smart_moves: Used pure file splitting and renaming without apparent semantic edits, keeping the PR focused on improving decomp organization while preserving existing assembly.
Kept link order explicit in obj_files.mk: lbarchive.o and lbdvd.o were inserted before the residual code_80019230.o where code_80016A54.o previously sat, reducing risk of address/order drift.
Used embedded source filename strings such as "lbarchive.c" and "lbdvd.c" as evidence for naming split assembly files after original C translation units.
Converted generic placeholder names into subsystem paths, especially moving text_1.s into asm/melee/gm/gmmain.s.
decomp_lessons: When a large asm blob contains multiple original source files, split it into source-named .s files first; this makes later C decompilation smaller and more searchable.
After splitting the front of an address-based blob, rename the remaining file to the next unsplit start address, as shown by code_80016A54.s becoming code_80019230.s.
Always update obj_files.mk in the same PR as assembly splits so the linker consumes the new objects in the intended order.
Generic files like text_1.s should be replaced with subsystem/source names once enough evidence exists; here the gm/gmmain.s name was introduced as a direct rename.
Embedded .asciz source filename strings are useful anchors for deciding assembly file boundaries and names.
naming_conventions: Library subsystem files under asm/melee/lb use lowercase original-source-style names such as lbarchive.s and lbdvd.s.
Game/system files under asm/melee/gm use lowercase names such as gmmain.s.
Residual unsplit assembly files retain address-based names like code_80019230.s, matching the new start address of the remaining blob.
Object entries in obj_files.mk mirror the asm path and basename, e.g. asm/melee/lb/lbarchive.s maps to $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/melee/lb/lbarchive.o.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Performed a 100% similarity rename from asm/melee/text_1.s to asm/melee/gm/gmmain.s, indicating no instruction-level matching change for that file.
Split the old code_80016A54.s blob by moving large contiguous chunks into new lbarchive.s and lbdvd.s files, while leaving later code in code_80019230.s.
Preserved build/link sequencing by expanding one old object entry into three ordered object entries rather than appending the new objects elsewhere.
The diff evidence is assembly-focused and does not show newly decompiled C or header/struct changes.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice, so there is no direct reviewer feedback to extract.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that decompile asm/melee/lb/lbarchive.s into C.
Find later PRs that decompile asm/melee/lb/lbdvd.s into C.
Find later PRs that replace asm/melee/gm/gmmain.s with C or add gmmain headers.
Search for further splits of asm/melee/lb/code_80019230.s after this PR.
